title: Avoid List.Contains lookups inside loops
messageFormat: This List.Contains call runs on every loop iteration; use a set for repeated membership tests.
summary: Membership testing on a List<T> is a linear scan. Doing it inside a loop turns the surrounding work into a quadratic cost that is invisible on small inputs and crippling on large ones. When the same collection is searched repeatedly, build a HashSet<T> once and test against it, turning each lookup into a constant-time operation.
